description: Each person secretly picks 3 random ingredients from the kitchen — the other has to cook a full dish using all of them — judge on taste and creativity
Walk the neighborhood and find something that starts with each letter of the alphabet before getting back — first to complete it wins
Pick a direction and walk with no destination — take turns deciding at every intersection — no phones for navigation
Both invent a completely original cocktail using only what is in the apartment — name it and pitch it like you are on Shark Tank
Find an outdoor court and run a first to 21 — make it competitive with actual rules and trash talk required
Both have one hour to build or prototype the most interesting thing you can using free tools — Claude / no-code / whatever you have
Both try to get Claude to do something it shouldn't within the same constraint — most impressive result wins
Both do the same online logic puzzle or cipher simultaneously — no hints — first correct solution wins
Both invent a sauce or condiment from scratch using only pantry items — taste test and rate each other's on 5 categories
Grab any ball and set up increasingly hard trick shots around the apartment or outside — each person gets 3 attempts per shot
Find a court and run full games or 21 against whoever is there
One person has 10 minutes to hide 10 items and write clues — other person solves it against the clock — then swap
Both start on the same random article and race to reach a predetermined target article using only internal links
Take a frisbee to a field and compete on distance accuracy and trick throws — invent your own scoring system
Both eat a small amount of something in the fridge and try to reverse engineer the full recipe from scratch — cook it and compare
Lay outside with a free stargazing app and compete to identify the most constellations and objects before going in
One person prepares 10 small tastes of increasingly weird pantry combinations — the other guesses every ingredient blindfolded
Run a proper tournament bracket with rules — best of 7 series per match — make it feel official
Both prep the same set of vegetables as fast as possible — judge on speed and quality of cuts
Both attempt the same medium difficulty coding problem simultaneously — first accepted solution wins — loser buys next round
Start a homemade hot sauce or fermentation project together — research the method and begin the build
Map out a 9-hole disc golf course through the neighborhood using trees lamp posts and benches as targets
Run a multi-game card tournament — Cribbage / Rummy / Spades — keep a running score across the night
Film yourselves making a ridiculous late night dish like you are hosting a cooking show — full commentary and presentation
Both have one hour and access to whatever is in the apartment to build the most functional or impressive physical object you can
Both have 15 minutes to prepare a full startup pitch — problem solution market size go to market — present to each other and grill with questions
Pull up a complex cipher or ARG puzzle online and race to crack it together against a timer
Set up a backyard toss game and run a proper tournament — first to 21 — no mercy rule
Both go on a walk with the same list of 10 abstract prompts to photograph — loneliness / power / chaos etc — judge each other's interpretations
Pull every hot sauce and condiment out of the fridge and build a definitive collaborative tier list — argue every placement
